allowed_claim: cuFlye can export a deterministic M6b read-to-graph input-boundary replay pack from the M6a oracle and replay the selected raw overlaps back into canonical chain_input rows without changing Flye graph logic.
forbidden_claim: M6b does not add CUDA, replace quickSeqOverlaps, feed replayed records into Flye, prove default GPU mode, or prove whole-Flye speedup.
next_recommended_step: M6c: consume the M6b replay pack with a CUDA raw-overlap filter/sort prototype and prove CUDA-produced chain_input rows canonical-diff match against oracle.chain-input.tsv for every selected query.
plain_language_benefit: M6b still has no direct CUDA speedup. Its benefit is that the next CUDA prototype now has a small, deterministic pack to consume: 8 selected queries, 36 raw overlaps, and 8 expected chain_input rows. The replay proves those expected rows can be reconstructed from raw overlap records before any GPU output is allowed near Flye graph logic.

missing_for_full_candidate_minimizer_gen: k-mer size and minimizer window parameters
sufficient_for: chain_input ABI production and canonical diff
